archetype: Judicial Record Sealing and Unsealing Orchestration System
description: A system that automates the cryptographic sealing, access-controlled unsealing, and immutable audit of court records according to varying jurisdictional rules. It serves court clerks, judges, and attorneys, with complexity arising from parsing 50+ state-specific sealing statutes, integrating with legacy case management systems (e.g., CM/ECF), and ensuring zero accidental disclosure during ephemeral access grants.
rules: MUST enforce rule evaluation against real statutes (e.g., FRCP Rule 5.2, specific state court rules) and local administrative orders.
MUST generate a cryptographically sealed, tamper-evident package for each record using NIST-approved algorithms upon sealing.
MUST log every access attempt, successful or not, to an immutable audit trail meeting ISO 27001 standards.
MUST integrate with existing court authentication systems (e.g., PACER, state CM systems) and never store credentials.
MUST automatically expire time-limited unsealing grants and re-seal records without manual intervention.
anti_patterns: DO NOT implement a single, uniform sealing policy—jurisdictional variations make this legally invalid.
DO NOT rely on database-level encryption alone; sealing must be a portable, self-contained cryptographic object.
DO NOT allow 'view-only' access without an explicit, time-bound unsealing grant and logged justification.
key_technical_challenges: Building a deterministic, auditable rule engine that correctly interprets and applies diverse, sometimes conflicting, legal sealing criteria from multiple sources.
Ensuring zero-knowledge proof of seal status to external systems without exposing record content or unsealing keys.
Achieving sub-second latency for seal/unseal operations while integrating with high-latency, legacy mainframe-based case management APIs.

recommended_stack.language: Java - Strong ecosystem for enterprise integration and cryptographic libraries used in government systems.
recommended_stack.framework: Spring Boot - Mature for building secure, transactional, API-driven services that integrate with legacy SOAP/XML court systems.
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL - ACID compliance for critical audit logs and support for pgcrypto extensions.
recommended_stack.cache: Redis - For short-lived, encrypted access tokens and session state during active unsealing.
recommended_stack.message_broker: Apache Kafka - For immutable, ordered audit event streams and rule update dissemination.

compliance_flags: State-Specific Court Rules (e.g., CA Rules of Court)
NIST SP 800-88 for Data Sanitization upon re-seal
